name: agent_handoff
description: an agent-to-human escalation world; actions handle, escalate, resolve

card.description: an agent-to-human escalation world; actions handle, escalate, resolve

rules: A case is owned by the agent until it is escalated to a human.
handle: the current owner works the case; the agent gains confidence with diminishing returns against complexity, a human becomes fully confident.
escalate: transfers ownership from the agent to a human and engages them; it has no effect once the case is already with a human.
resolve: the agent may close the case only when its confidence is at least the case's complexity; an engaged human may always close the case.
Every action consumes one unit of the SLA budget.
A resolved case is terminal and ignores all further actions.
If the SLA budget reaches zero before resolution, the case is breached.

perception.code:
def perceive(data):
    if isinstance(data, dict):
        text = str(data.get("text", ""))
        words = int(data.get("words", len(text.split())))
        priority = str(data.get("priority", "normal"))
    else:
        text = str(data)
        words = len(text.split())
        priority = "normal"
    # Longer tickets read as more complex; the agent's confidence is the inverse.
    complexity = round(min(1.0, max(0.0, words / 200.0)), 3)
    confidence = round(max(0.0, 1.0 - complexity), 3)
    sla_remaining = 4 if priority == "urgent" else 8
    return {"complexity": complexity, "confidence": confidence, "sla_remaining": sla_remaining}

emit.report: Case is {status} (owner: {owner}). confidence={confidence} vs complexity={complexity} after {attempts} attempt(s) and {escalations} escalation(s); sla_remaining={sla_remaining}, resolved={resolved}, breached={breached}.
